requiredAssets.note: Use the official white wordmark on dark surfaces.
Use official restaurant photography before generated imagery.
Menu facts must come from official menu source.

warnings: Official menu extraction was cleaned before build; prices are shown only when source confidence is clear.
rules: Website route first: sell the restaurant experience, not just the menu.
Use real logo and real restaurant photos.
Use dark steakhouse atmosphere with ivory/brass contrast.
Keep phone, map, and booking links mobile-clickable.
Menu route can exist, but homepage must read as a formal official website.

typography.heading: Montserrat
typography.numeric: Tabular figures for ratings and prices

directions.name: Premium Steakhouse Editorial
directions.philosophy: Swiss editorial structure with warm steakhouse materiality
directions.bestFor: A steak and seafood restaurant that already has strong official photography and wants a more composed, premium digital presence.
directions.menuTreatment: Homepage shows selected official menu proof; full menu remains a secondary mobile-friendly route.

layoutRules: First viewport must show logo, steak/seafood positioning, rating proof, and booking CTA.
Use one immersive hero photo and one editorial image panel; avoid generic cards.
Functions/private dining gets a dedicated section because the official site emphasizes it.
Desktop layout may be asymmetrical; mobile must become a clear single-column booking path.
Sticky mobile actions should make Reserve, Map, and Call immediately available.
